steps.title: Let Them Overextend
steps.text: The Grünfeld (1.d4 Nf6 2.c4 g6 3.Nc3 d5) is the counter-puncher's dream. Black lets White build a massive center and then attacks it with queenside pieces and pawn breaks.

steps.title: The Exchange Variation
steps.text: After 4.cxd5 Nxd5 5.e4, Black's knight retreats and White has a huge center — but Black threatens to destroy it with pieces. A key test of dynamic chess: static center vs. piece activity.

steps.title: Classical Grünfeld Themes
steps.text: Black's plans: fianchetto on g7, pressure d4 with ...c5, castle, ...Nc6, ...Bg4. The fianchettoed bishop on g7 is key — it radiates power into White's position, especially after ...d4 breakdowns.
